title: AccuracyAuthorization.Full
text: Full accuracy authorized.

title: AccuracyAuthorization.Reduced
text: Reduced accuracy granted.

title: ActivityConfig.activityRecognitionInterval
text: Interval in milliseconds between motion-activity polls. Defaults to ms (10 seconds). Lower values increase responsiveness to state changes at the cost of additional battery use. The minimum supported  activityRecognitionInterval?: number;

title: ActivityConfig.disableMotionActivityUpdates
text: Disables the SDK's request for Motion & Fitness (iOS) or Physical Activity (Android 10+) permission. Defaults to . Set to to suppress the permission request. The SDK remains functional but without mot disableMotionActivityUpdates?: boolean;

title: ActivityConfig.disableStopDetection
text: Disables the motion-activity-based stop-detection system. Defaults to . iOS Disables the accelerometer-based stop-detection system. When disabled, the SDK falls back to the default iOS behaviour of au disableStopDetection?: boolean;

title: ActivityConfig.minimumActivityRecognitionConfidence
text: Minimum confidence level (0–100) required before a detected activity triggers a moving/stationary state change. Defaults to on iOS and on Android. Higher values reduce false-positive transitions but m minimumActivityRecognitionConfidence?: number;

title: ActivityConfig.motionTriggerDelay
text: [Android only] Adds a delay in milliseconds before the SDK commits to the *moving* state when the Motion API reports movement. Defaults to (no delay). When the Motion API reports a moving activity (e. motionTriggerDelay?: number;

title: ActivityConfig.stopDetectionDelay
text: [iOS only] Delays the iOS stop-detection system from engaging after the device becomes stationary. Defaults to (engage immediately). When the stop-detection system engages, location services are tempo stopDetectionDelay?: number;

title: ActivityConfig.stopOnStationary
text: Automatically calls { stopOnStationary?: boolean;

title: ActivityConfig.triggerActivities
text: Restricts which detected activities can trigger a transition from the *stationary* state to the *moving* state. By default the SDK triggers on any moving activity: , , , , or . Configure this to limit triggerActivities?: TriggerActivity | TriggerActivity[];

title: ActivityType.Airborne
text: Airborne activity (iOS 15+).

title: ActivityType.AutomotiveNavigation
text: Automotive navigation mode.

title: ActivityType.Fitness
text: Fitness-related activity.

title: ActivityType.Other
text: Default/unspecified activity.

title: ActivityType.OtherNavigation
text: Non-automotive navigation mode.

title: AppConfig.backgroundPermissionRationale
text: [Android only] Configures the dialog shown when requesting *Always* background location permission on Android 11 and later. Android 11 removed the Allow all the time button from the system permission  backgroundPermissionRationale?: PermissionRationale;

title: AppConfig.enableHeadless
text: [Android only] Enables headless operation so the SDK can respond to events after the app has been terminated with { enableHeadless?: boolean;

title: AppConfig.heartbeatInterval
text: Rate in seconds at which { heartbeatInterval?: number;

title: AppConfig.notification
text: [Android only] Configures the persistent foreground-service notification required by Android. ![](https://dl.dropbox.com/s/acuhy5cu4p7uofr/android-foreground-service-default.png?dl=1) See { notification?: NotificationConfig;

title: AppConfig.preventSuspend
text: [iOS only] Prevents iOS from suspending the application after location services have been disabled while running in the background. Defaults to . Set to to keep the app alive in the background even af preventSuspend?: boolean;

title: AppConfig.schedule
text: Configures a cron-like automated schedule that tells the SDK when to { schedule?: string[];

title: AppConfig.scheduleUseAlarmManager
text: [Android only] Forces the Android scheduler to use (exact, on-the-minute) instead of (inexact). Defaults to . scheduleUseAlarmManager?: boolean;

title: AppConfig.startOnBoot
text: Controls whether tracking resumes automatically after the device reboots. Defaults to . iOS iOS cannot begin tracking immediately after a reboot. Similar to { startOnBoot?: boolean;

title: AppConfig.stopOnTerminate
text: Controls whether tracking stops when the application is terminated by the user. Defaults to . When , the SDK calls as the app terminates. Set to to keep tracking alive after termination — iOS and Andr stopOnTerminate?: boolean;

title: AuthorizationConfig.accessToken
text: The access token attached to each HTTP request as an header. The SDK automatically applies the token to every upload request sent to { accessToken: string;

title: AuthorizationConfig.expires
text: Token expiry time in seconds since epoch. Defaults to (not set). When set, the SDK proactively refreshes the token before expiry rather than waiting for a response. When not set (default ), the SDK re expires?: number;

title: AuthorizationConfig.refreshHeaders
text: HTTP headers sent with each request to { refreshHeaders?: Record<string, string>;

title: AuthorizationConfig.refreshPayload
text: Form fields sent in the POST to { refreshPayload?: Record<string, string>;

title: AuthorizationConfig.refreshToken
text: The token sent to { refreshToken?: string;

title: AuthorizationConfig.refreshUrl
text: The URL of the authorization server that provides a new { refreshUrl?: string;

title: AuthorizationConfig.strategy
text: Authorization strategy used when attaching the token to HTTP requests. Defaults to . - — sends the token as . - — sends the token as (no prefix). strategy: string;

title: AuthorizationEvent.error
text: Error message when is ; otherwise. error: string;

title: AuthorizationEvent.response
text: Decoded JSON body returned by the refresh server when is ; otherwise. response: any;

title: AuthorizationEvent.status
text: HTTP status code returned by { status: number;

title: AuthorizationEvent.success
text: when the token refresh request returned a 2xx status code. success: boolean;

title: AuthorizationStatus.Always
text: Location permissions authorized for always use.

title: AuthorizationStatus.Denied
text: Location permissions denied by the user.

title: AuthorizationStatus.NotDetermined
text: User has not yet made a choice regarding location permissions.

title: AuthorizationStatus.Restricted
text: Location permissions are restricted (e.g., parental controls).

title: AuthorizationStatus.WhenInUse
text: Location permissions authorized for when-in-use (Android 10+).

title: AuthorizationStrategy.Jwt
text: JWT-based authorization with accessToken and refreshToken. Access token is sent in the Authorization header of each request. Refresh token is used to obtain new access tokens when they expire.

title: AuthorizationStrategy.Sas
text: Shared Access Signature (SAS) token-based authorization. SAS token is sent in the Authorization header of each request. Client is responsible for refreshing the SAS token before it expires.

title: BackgroundGeolocation.AccuracyAuthorization
text: Enum namespace indicating whether the user granted full or reduced location accuracy. [iOS 14+] Used by and . AccuracyAuthorization: object;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.ActivityType
text: [iOS only] Enum namespace specifying the type of user activity (AutomotiveNavigation, Fitness, OtherNavigation, etc). Used by { ActivityType: object;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.AuthorizationStatus
text: Enum namespace representing the OS-level authorization state for location services (Denied, Restricted, Always, WhenInUse). Returned from and . AuthorizationStatus: object;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.AuthorizationStrategy
text: Enum namespace defining how the HTTP service performs authorization. Includes basic, JWT, and custom strategies. Used by . AuthorizationStrategy: object;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.DesiredAccuracy
text: Enum namespace controlling the native location engine's target accuracy. Higher accuracy consumes more battery. Used by . DesiredAccuracy: object;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.Event
text: Enum namespace of all event names emitted by the SDK (, , , , etc). Event: object;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.HttpMethod
text: Enum namespace defining the HTTP method used for location uploads (POST, PUT, etc). Used by . HttpMethod: object;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.KalmanProfile
text: Enum namespace selecting a preset Kalman filter tuning profile (aggressive, moderate, or relaxed smoothing). Used by . KalmanProfile: object;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.LocationFilterPolicy
text: Enum namespace selecting the filtering engine policy for noise-reduction and smoothing. Used by . LocationFilterPolicy: object;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.LocationRequest
text: [iOS only] Enum namespace defining the type of location permission request (Always, WhenInUse, or Any). Used by . LocationRequest: object;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.LogLevel
text: Enum namespace controlling verbosity of the SDK logger. Used by . Values range from silent () to extremely verbose (). LogLevel: object;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.NotificationPriority
text: [Android only] Enum namespace controlling Android foreground-service notification priority and icon placement (top, bottom, hidden). Used by . NotificationPriority: object;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.PersistMode
text: Enum namespace controlling which records the SDK persists to SQLite: locations only, geofences only, both, or none. Used by . PersistMode: object;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.TriggerActivity
text: Enum namespace defining which physical motion activities can trigger motion-detection transitions (still → moving). Used by . TriggerActivity: object;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.addGeofence
text: Add a { addGeofence(geofence: Geofence): Promise<boolean>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.addGeofences
text: Add a list of { addGeofences(geofences: Geofence[]): Promise<boolean>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.changePace
text: Manually toggle the SDK's motion state between stationary and moving. Passing immediately engages location services and begins tracking, bypassing stationary monitoring. Passing turns off location ser changePace(isMoving: boolean): Promise<State>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.destroyLocation
text: Remove a single location by { destroyLocation(uuid: string): Promise<void>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.destroyLocations
text: Remove all records from the SDK's SQLite database. destroyLocations(): Promise<void>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.destroyTransistorAuthorizationToken
text: Destroy a Transistor authorization token. See { destroyTransistorAuthorizationToken(url: string): Promise<void>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.deviceSettings
text: { deviceSettings: DeviceSettings;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.findOrCreateTransistorAuthorizationToken
text: Find or create a Transistor authorization token. See { findOrCreateTransistorAuthorizationToken(orgname: string, username: string, url?: string): Promise<TransistorAuthorizationToken>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.geofenceExists
text: Determine whether a geofence with the given identifier exists in the SDK's database. See also - 📘 { geofenceExists(identifier: string): Promise<boolean>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.getCount
text: Retrieve the count of all locations currently stored in the SDK's SQLite database. getCount(): Promise<number>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.getCurrentPosition
text: Retrieve the current { getCurrentPosition(options?: CurrentPositionRequest): Promise<Location>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.getDeviceInfo
text: Returns device information. getDeviceInfo(): Promise<DeviceInfo>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.getGeofence
text: Fetch a single { getGeofence(identifier: string): Promise<Geofence>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.getGeofences
text: Fetch all { getGeofences(): Promise<Geofence[]>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.getLocations
text: Retrieve all { getLocations(): Promise<Object[]>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.getOdometer
text: Retrieve the current odometer reading in meters. The SDK continuously accumulates distance traveled between recorded locations. ⚠️ Warning Odometer accuracy depends on location accuracy. Noisy or inac getOdometer(): Promise<number>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.getProviderState
text: Retrieve the current location-services authorization state. See also - { getProviderState(): Promise<ProviderChangeEvent>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.getSensors
text: Returns the availability of motion sensors: accelerometer, gyroscope, and magnetometer. These sensors power the motion activity-recognition system. When any sensor is absent (particularly on low-end A getSensors(): Promise<Sensors>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.getState
text: Return the current { getState(): Promise<State>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.isPowerSaveMode
text: Returns the current state of the operating system's power-saving mode. Power-saving mode can throttle background services such as GPS and HTTP uploads. See also - { isPowerSaveMode(): Promise<boolean>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.logger
text: { logger: Logger;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.onActivityChange
text: Subscribe to motion-activity changes. Fires each time the activity-recognition system reports a new activity (, , , , ). Android { onActivityChange(cb: (event: MotionActivityEvent) => void): Subscription;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.onAuthorization
text: Subscribe to { onAuthorization(cb: (event: AuthorizationEvent) => void): Subscription;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.onConnectivityChange
text: Subscribe to network connectivity changes. Fires when the device's network connectivity transitions between connected and disconnected. By default, the SDK also fires this event at { onConnectivityChange(cb: (event: ConnectivityChangeEvent) => void): Subscription;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.onEnabledChange
text: Subscribe to changes in plugin { onEnabledChange(cb: (enabled: boolean) => void): Subscription;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.onGeofence
text: Subscribe to geofence transition events. Fires when any monitored geofence crossing occurs. See also - 📘 { onGeofence(cb: (event: GeofenceEvent) => void): Subscription;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.onGeofencesChange
text: Subscribe to changes in the set of actively monitored geofences. Fires when the SDK's active geofence set changes. The SDK can monitor any number of geofences in its database — even thousands — despit onGeofencesChange(cb: (event: GeofencesChangeEvent) => void): Subscription;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.onHeartbeat
text: Subscribe to periodic heartbeat events. Fires at each { onHeartbeat(cb: (event: HeartbeatEvent) => void): Subscription;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.onHttp
text: Subscribe to HTTP responses from your server { onHttp(cb: (event: HttpEvent) => void): Subscription;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.onLocation
text: Subscribe to location events. Every location recorded by the SDK is delivered to your callback, including locations from { onLocation(cb: (location: Location) => void, onError?: (err: LocationError) => void): Subscription;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.onMotionChange
text: Subscribe to motion-change events. Fires each time the device transitions between the moving and stationary states. ⚠️ Warning When a motion-change event fires, { onMotionChange(cb: (event: MotionChangeEvent) => void): Subscription;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.onNotificationAction
text: [Android only] Subscribe to button-click actions on the Android foreground-service notification. Fires when the user taps a button defined in a custom { onNotificationAction(cb: (buttonId: string) => void): Subscription;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.onPowerSaveChange
text: Subscribe to OS power-saving mode changes. Fires when the operating system's power-saving mode is enabled or disabled. Power-saving mode can throttle background services such as GPS and HTTP uploads.  onPowerSaveChange(cb: (enabled: boolean) => void): Subscription;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.onProviderChange
text: Subscribe to location-services authorization changes. Fires whenever the state of the device's location-services authorization changes (e.g. GPS enabled, WiFi-only, permission revoked). The SDK also f onProviderChange(cb: (event: ProviderChangeEvent) => void): Subscription;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.onSchedule
text: Subscribe to { onSchedule(cb: (state: State) => void): Subscription;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.playSound
text: Play a system sound. iOS Provide a numeric . Android Provide a sound name string. playSound(soundId: string | number): void;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.ready
text: Signal to the SDK that your app is launched and ready, supplying the default { ready(config: Config): Promise<State>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.registerHeadlessTask
text: [Android only] Registers a headless-task callback for Android background events when the app has been terminated with { registerHeadlessTask(callback: (event: HeadlessEvent) => Promise<void>): void;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.removeGeofence
text: Remove the { removeGeofence(identifier: string): Promise<boolean>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.removeGeofences
text: Remove all monitored { removeGeofences(identifiers?: string[]): Promise<boolean>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.removeListeners
text: Remove all event listeners. Calls { removeListeners(): Promise<void>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.requestPermission
text: Manually request location permission using the configured { requestPermission(): Promise<AuthorizationStatus>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.requestTemporaryFullAccuracy
text: Request temporary full-accuracy location authorization. [iOS 14+] iOS 14 allows users to grant only reduced location accuracy. This method presents the system dialog ([](https://developer.apple.com/do requestTemporaryFullAccuracy(purposeKey: string): Promise<AccuracyAuthorization>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.reset
text: Reset the SDK configuration to documented default values. If an optional { reset(config: Config): Promise<State>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.resetOdometer
text: Reset the odometer to . Internally performs a { resetOdometer(): Promise<number>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.setConfig
text: Update the SDK's { setConfig(config: Partial<Config>): Promise<State>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.setLogLevel
text: Sets the { setLogLevel(level: LogLevel): Promise<void>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.setOdometer
text: Set the odometer to an arbitrary value. Internally performs a { setOdometer(value: number): Promise<number>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.start
text: Enable location and geofence tracking. This is the SDK's power ON switch. The SDK enters its stationary state, acquires an initial location, then turns off location services until motion is detected.  start(): Promise<State>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.startBackgroundTask
text: Signal to the OS that you need to perform a long-running task. The OS keeps the app running in the background until you signal completion with { startBackgroundTask(): Promise<number>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.startGeofences
text: Switch to geofences-only tracking mode. In this mode no active location tracking occurs — only geofences are monitored. Use the usual { startGeofences(): Promise<State>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.startSchedule
text: Activate the configured { startSchedule(): Promise<void>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.stop
text: Disable location and geofence monitoring. This is the SDK's power OFF switch. Note If a { stop(): Promise<State>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.stopBackgroundTask
text: Signal completion of a { stopBackgroundTask(taskId: number): Promise<void>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.stopSchedule
text: Halt scheduled tracking. ⚠️ Warning does not call { stopSchedule(): Promise<void>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.sync
text: Manually upload all queued locations to { sync(): Promise<Object[]>;

title: BackgroundGeolocation.watchPosition
text: Start a continuous stream of location updates. Each location is persisted to SQLite (when the SDK is { watchPosition(options: WatchPositionRequest, locationCallback: (location: Location) => void, errorCallback?: (errorCode: number) => void): Subscription;

title: Battery.is_charging
text: when the device is connected to a power source. is_charging: boolean;

title: Battery.level
text: Battery charge level: = empty, = fully charged. level: number;

title: Config.activity
text: Motion recognition, stop-detection triggers, and motion-trigger delay. Access via . See { activity?: ActivityConfig;

title: Config.app
text: App lifecycle, background operation, boot behaviour, headless mode, and foreground notification. Access via . See { app?: AppConfig;

title: Config.authorization
text: JWT and SAS token management with automatic refresh. Access via . See { authorization?: AuthorizationConfig;

title: Config.geolocation
text: Accuracy, sampling, elasticity, stop-detection, permissions, and geofencing. Access via . See { geolocation?: GeoConfig;

title: Config.http
text: Upload URL, HTTP method, sync cadence, batching, headers, and params. Access via . See { http?: HttpConfig;

title: Config.logger
text: Debug logging, log level, and log retention. Access via . See { logger?: LoggerConfig;

title: Config.persistence
text: SQLite storage, TTL, record limits, and custom extras. Access via . See { persistence?: PersistenceConfig;

title: Config.reset
text: Controls whether the SDK resets to factory defaults before applying this configuration. Defaults to . When (the default), every call to { reset?: boolean;

title: Config.transistorAuthorizationToken
text: Convenience option that automatically configures the SDK to upload locations to the Transistor Software demo server at tracker.transistorsoft.com, or a local instance of [background-geolocation-consol transistorAuthorizationToken?: TransistorAuthorizationToken;

title: ConnectivityChangeEvent.connected
text: when the device has network connectivity; when it is offline. connected: boolean;

title: Coords.accuracy
text: Horizontal accuracy radius in meters. accuracy: number;

title: Coords.altitude
text: Altitude above a reference plane in meters. iOS Altitude above mean sea level. Android Altitude above the WGS84 reference ellipsoid. See also { altitude?: number;

title: Coords.altitude_accuracy
text: Vertical accuracy in meters. Returns if unavailable. iOS When positive, the value is within ± meters. When negative, is invalid. Determining altitude accuracy requires a GPS-capable device; on some de altitude_accuracy?: number;

title: Coords.ellipsoidal_altitude
text: Altitude above the WGS84 reference ellipsoid in meters. ellipsoidal_altitude?: number;

title: Coords.floor
text: [iOS only] Floor within a building, when indoor-positioning hardware (e.g. Bluetooth beacons) is available. floor?: number;

title: Coords.heading
text: Direction of travel in degrees (0–360, clockwise from true north). Note Only present when the location came from GPS. Returns otherwise. heading?: number;

title: Coords.heading_accuracy
text: Heading accuracy in degrees. Note Only present when the location came from GPS. Returns otherwise. heading_accuracy?: number;

title: Coords.latitude
text: Latitude in decimal degrees. latitude: number;

title: Coords.longitude
text: Longitude in decimal degrees. longitude: number;

title: Coords.speed
text: Ground speed in meters per second. Note Only present when the location came from GPS. Returns otherwise. speed?: number;

title: Coords.speed_accuracy
text: Speed accuracy in meters per second. Note Only present when the location came from GPS. Returns otherwise. speed_accuracy?: number;

title: CurrentPositionRequest.desiredAccuracy
text: Accuracy threshold in meters. The SDK stops sampling as soon as it receives a location with and returns it immediately. Defaults to { desiredAccuracy?: number;

title: CurrentPositionRequest.extras
text: Optional key-value metadata to attach to the returned location. Merged with any configured { extras?: Record<string, any>;

title: CurrentPositionRequest.maximumAge
text: Accept the most recently recorded location if it is no older than this value in milliseconds. Default (always fetch a fresh fix). maximumAge?: number;

title: CurrentPositionRequest.persist
text: Whether to persist the returned location to the SDK's SQLite database and upload it to { persist?: boolean;

title: CurrentPositionRequest.samples
text: Maximum number of location samples to collect before returning the most accurate result. Default . Only the final selected location is persisted. samples?: number;

title: CurrentPositionRequest.timeout
text: Maximum time in seconds to wait for a location fix. Default . If the timeout expires before a satisfactory location is found, the Promise rejects with a { timeout?: number;

title: DesiredAccuracy.High
text: High accuracy (e.g., 0-10m).

title: DesiredAccuracy.Low
text: Low accuracy (e.g., 100m).

title: DesiredAccuracy.Lowest
text: Lowest accuracy (e.g., 3000m).

title: DesiredAccuracy.Medium
text: Medium accuracy (e.g., 10m).

title: DesiredAccuracy.Navigation
text: Navigation-level accuracy (e.g., 0-10m).

title: DesiredAccuracy.VeryLow
text: Very low accuracy (e.g., 1000m).

title: DeviceInfo.framework
text: Development framework hosting the SDK. Examples: , , , framework: string;

title: DeviceInfo.manufacturer
text: Device manufacturer. Examples: , , , manufacturer: string;

title: DeviceInfo.model
text: Device model identifier. Examples: , , model: string;

title: DeviceInfo.platform
text: OS platform name: or . platform: string;

title: DeviceInfo.version
text: OS version string. Examples: , , version: string;

title: DeviceSettings.isIgnoringBatteryOptimizations
text: [Android only] Returns if the OS is ignoring battery optimizations for your app. In most cases the SDK performs acceptably even when battery optimizations are active, but aggressive manufacturer-speci isIgnoringBatteryOptimizations(): Promise<boolean>;

title: DeviceSettings.show
text: [Android only] Execute a previously prepared { show(request: DeviceSettingsRequest): Promise<boolean>;

title: DeviceSettings.showIgnoreBatteryOptimizations
text: [Android only] Prepare a request to show the Android *Ignore Battery Optimizations* settings screen. Returns a { showIgnoreBatteryOptimizations(): Promise<DeviceSettingsRequest>;

title: DeviceSettings.showPowerManager
text: [Android only] Prepare a request to show a vendor-specific Power Manager settings screen (Huawei, Xiaomi, Vivo, Oppo, etc.). Returns a { showPowerManager(): Promise<DeviceSettingsRequest>;

title: DeviceSettingsRequest.action
text: The Android Intent action used to open the target settings screen. ⚠️ Warning This field is set automatically by the native layer. Do not set it manually. action: string;

title: DeviceSettingsRequest.lastSeenAt
text: Timestamp of the last time this screen was shown to the user. lastSeenAt: Date;

title: DeviceSettingsRequest.manufacturer
text: Device manufacturer (e.g. , ). manufacturer: string;

title: DeviceSettingsRequest.model
text: Device model name (e.g. , ). model: string;

title: DeviceSettingsRequest.seen
text: if this settings screen has already been shown to the user on this device. Use this to avoid showing the same prompt repeatedly. seen: boolean;

title: DeviceSettingsRequest.version
text: Android OS version string. version: string;

title: Event.ActivityChange
text: Emitted when a motion-activity change is detected.

title: Event.Authorization
text: Emitted when the plugin's authorization state changes.

title: Event.Boot
text: Emitted when the plugin boots.

title: Event.ConnectivityChange
text: Emitted when the device's network connectivity changes.

title: Event.EnabledChange
text: Emitted when the plugin's enabled-state changes.

title: Event.Geofence
text: Emitted when a geofence transition occurs.

title: Event.GeofencesChange
text: Emitted when geofences are added/removed/activated/deactivated.

title: Event.Heartbeat
text: Emitted at the configured when the plugin is running.

title: Event.Http
text: Emitted when an HTTP request is sent to the configured endpoint.

title: Event.Location
text: Emitted when a location is recorded.

title: Event.MotionChange
text: Emitted when a motion-change is detected.

title: Event.Notification
text: Emitted when a user interacts with a plugin-generated notification.

title: Event.PowerSaveChange
text: Emitted when the device enters or exits power-saving mode.

title: Event.ProviderChange
text: Emitted when the device location-provider changes state.

title: Event.Schedule
text: Emitted when a configured schedule is triggered.

title: Event.Terminate
text: Emitted when the plugin is terminated.

title: GeoConfig.activityType
text: [iOS only] Specifies the Core Motion activity type used by iOS to optimize its internal stop-detection algorithm. Apple is intentionally vague about how this affects motion interpretation, but each ac activityType?: ActivityType;

title: GeoConfig.allowIdenticalLocations
text: [Android only] Allows duplicate locations to be recorded when consecutive fixes are identical. Defaults to . By default, the SDK ignores a location that is identical to the previous one. Set to record allowIdenticalLocations?: boolean;

title: GeoConfig.deferTime
text: [Android only] Sets the maximum wait time in milliseconds before batched location updates are delivered. Defaults to (no deferral). When set to a value at least 2× the { deferTime?: number;

title: GeoConfig.desiredAccuracy
text: Specifies the desired accuracy of the geolocation system. Defaults to { desiredAccuracy?: DesiredAccuracy;

title: GeoConfig.disableElasticity
text: Disables automatic speed-based { disableElasticity?: boolean;

title: GeoConfig.disableLocationAuthorizationAlert
text: Disables the SDK's automatic alert when location authorization is insufficient. Defaults to . By default, the SDK shows a native alert directing the user to the Settings screen when location services  disableLocationAuthorizationAlert?: boolean;

title: GeoConfig.disableStopDetection
text: Disables the motion-activity–based stop-detection system. When , the SDK ignores platform motion-activity signals when determining whether the device is stationary. This affects how and when location  disableStopDetection?: boolean;

title: GeoConfig.distanceFilter
text: The minimum distance (meters) a device must move horizontally before a new location is recorded. Defaults to meters. By default, is elastically auto-scaled by the SDK: when speed increases, increases; distanceFilter?: number;

title: GeoConfig.elasticityMultiplier
text: Controls the scale of automatic speed-based { elasticityMultiplier?: number;

title: GeoConfig.enableTimestampMeta
text: Appends extra timestamp metadata to each recorded location, including system time. Defaults to . Some devices report GPS { enableTimestampMeta?: boolean;

title: GeoConfig.fastestLocationUpdateInterval
text: [Android only] Sets the fastest interval for location updates, in milliseconds. Defaults to (not set). When other apps or system components trigger location updates at a faster rate, the SDK receives  fastestLocationUpdateInterval?: number;

title: GeoConfig.filter
text: Defines how raw GPS samples are filtered, denoised, and smoothed before being recorded or used for odometer calculations. is supplied via { filter?: LocationFilter;

title: GeoConfig.geofenceInitialTriggerEntry
text: Controls whether a geofence fires an entry event immediately if the device is already inside it when the geofence is registered. Defaults to . Set to suppress the immediate entry trigger and wait unti geofenceInitialTriggerEntry?: boolean;

title: GeoConfig.geofenceModeHighAccuracy
text: [Android only] Enables high-accuracy mode for geofence-only tracking. Defaults to . Runs { geofenceModeHighAccuracy?: boolean;

title: GeoConfig.geofenceProximityRadius
text: Defines the radius (meters) around the device used to query for geofences that should be actively monitored. Mobile platforms allow only a limited number of concurrently monitored geofences (iOS: 20,  geofenceProximityRadius?: number;

title: GeoConfig.locationAuthorizationAlert
text: [iOS only] Customizes the text displayed in the SDK's location-authorization alert dialog. When { locationAuthorizationAlert?: Record<string, any>;

title: GeoConfig.locationAuthorizationRequest
text: Declares the location authorization level the app requires from the user. Defaults to . Valid values are: - — background and foreground location access - — foreground location access only - — accept w locationAuthorizationRequest?: "Always" | "WhenInUse" | "Any";

title: GeoConfig.locationUpdateInterval
text: [Android only] Sets the desired interval for active location updates, in milliseconds. Defaults to ms. The system honors this interval on a best-effort basis — updates may arrive slower (no providers  locationUpdateInterval?: number;

title: GeoConfig.pausesLocationUpdatesAutomatically
text: [iOS only] Prevents the iOS location API from automatically pausing location updates. When set to , iOS location services never turn off automatically. By default (), the SDK manages this automaticall pausesLocationUpdatesAutomatically?: boolean;

title: GeoConfig.showsBackgroundLocationIndicator
text: [iOS only] Controls whether the status bar indicator appears when location services are active in the background with authorization. Defaults to . When , iOS displays a blue bar or pill in the status  showsBackgroundLocationIndicator?: boolean;

title: GeoConfig.stationaryRadius
text: The minimum distance the device must move beyond the stationary location before aggressive background-tracking re-engages. iOS Defaults to meters. In practice, iOS requires approximately 200 meters of stationaryRadius?: number;

title: GeoConfig.stopAfterElapsedMinutes
text: Automatically stops tracking after the specified number of minutes. Disabled by default. When set, the SDK automatically calls { stopAfterElapsedMinutes?: number;

title: GeoConfig.stopOnStationary
text: Automatically calls { stopOnStationary?: boolean;

title: GeoConfig.stopTimeout
text: Minutes to wait in the *moving* state with no detected movement before transitioning to the *stationary* state. Defaults to minutes. When in the *moving* state, the SDK waits this many minutes after t stopTimeout?: number;

title: GeoConfig.useSignificantChangesOnly
text: Disables continuous background tracking in favour of periodic, coarse location updates. Defaults to . When , a location is recorded only every 500–1000 meters (higher in non-urban environments, depend useSignificantChangesOnly?: boolean;

title: Geofence.entryState
text: Current entry state of the geofence. | Value | State | |------:|-------| | | Outside | | | Inside | entryState?: number;

title: Geofence.extras
text: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to each geofence event and included in the payload posted to { extras?: Record<string, unknown>;

title: Geofence.hits
text: Number of times this geofence has been triggered since it was added. hits?: number;

title: Geofence.identifier
text: Unique identifier for this geofence. Used to reference the geofence in events and removal calls. Adding a geofence with an identifier that already exists replaces the existing one. identifier: string;

title: Geofence.latitude
text: Latitude of the circular geofence center. Omit when defining a polygon geofence via { latitude: number;

title: Geofence.loiteringDelay
text: Minimum time in milliseconds the device must remain inside the geofence before a { loiteringDelay?: number;

title: Geofence.longitude
text: Longitude of the circular geofence center. Omit when defining a polygon geofence via { longitude: number;

title: Geofence.notifyOnDwell
text: Fire a { notifyOnDwell?: boolean;

title: Geofence.notifyOnEntry
text: Fire a { notifyOnEntry?: boolean;

title: Geofence.notifyOnExit
text: Fire a { notifyOnExit?: boolean;

title: Geofence.radius
text: Radius of the circular geofence in meters. Omit when defining a polygon geofence via { radius: number;

title: Geofence.stateUpdatedAt
text: Epoch timestamp in seconds of the last geofence transition. stateUpdatedAt?: number;

title: Geofence.vertices
text: Polygon geofence vertices as pairs. When provided, the geofence is treated as a polygon rather than a circle. Omit { vertices?: Vertices;

title: GeofenceAction.Dwell
text: Device dwelled inside the geofence for at least the configured .

title: GeofenceAction.Enter
text: Device entered the geofence.

title: GeofenceAction.Exit
text: Device exited the geofence.

title: GeofenceEvent.action
text: Transition type that fired: , , or . action: GeofenceAction;

title: GeofenceEvent.extras
text: Optional metadata originally configured on the { extras?: Record<string, any>;

title: GeofenceEvent.geofence
text: The complete { geofence?: Geofence;

title: GeofenceEvent.identifier
text: The { identifier: string;

title: GeofenceEvent.location
text: Location recorded at the moment the geofence boundary was crossed. location: Location;

title: GeofenceEvent.timestamp
text: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp from the device system clock at the moment the OS delivered the geofence event. timestamp: string;

title: GeofencesChangeEvent.off
text: Identifiers of geofences that were just deactivated and are no longer being monitored. off: string[];

title: GeofencesChangeEvent.on
text: Geofences that just became active and are now being monitored. on: Geofence[];

title: HeadlessEvent.name
text: The SDK event that triggered this headless task invocation (e.g. , , , , ). name: Event;

title: HeadlessEvent.params
text: Event payload corresponding to . Cast this to the appropriate event type based on the value of (e.g. { params: Record<string, any>;

title: HeartbeatEvent.location
text: Most recent location recorded by the SDK. The heartbeat event does not actively request a fresh location fix. Call { location: Location;

title: HttpConfig.autoSync
text: Uploads each recorded location to { autoSync?: boolean;

title: HttpConfig.autoSyncThreshold
text: Minimum number of queued records required before an automatic upload fires. Defaults to (no threshold — upload after every recorded location). When set above , the SDK waits until at least this many l autoSyncThreshold?: number;

title: HttpConfig.batchSync
text: Bundles all queued locations into a single HTTP request. Defaults to . When , the SDK sends all records currently in the SQLite queue in one HTTP request rather than one request per location. Use { batchSync?: boolean;

title: HttpConfig.disableAutoSyncOnCellular
text: Defers automatic uploads until the device is on Wi-Fi. Defaults to . When , { disableAutoSyncOnCellular?: boolean;

title: HttpConfig.headers
text: HTTP headers applied to every outbound upload request. The supplied headers are merged with the SDK's automatically applied headers (including ). When { headers?: Record<string, string>;

title: HttpConfig.maxBatchSize
text: Maximum number of records included in each batched HTTP request. Defaults to (no limit — all queued records in one request). When { maxBatchSize?: number;

title: HttpConfig.method
text: The HTTP method used when uploading locations to { method?: HttpMethod;

title: HttpConfig.params
text: Key/value pairs merged into the JSON body of every outbound upload request. Params are merged at the root level of the payload, or nested under { params?: Record<string, any>;

title: HttpConfig.rootProperty
text: Wraps the location payload under a named root key in the JSON body. Defaults to . When set, outgoing payloads nest the serialized rootProperty?: string;

title: HttpConfig.timeout
text: HTTP request timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to ms (60 seconds). When a request exceeds this duration, the SDK fires { timeout?: number;

title: HttpConfig.url
text: The server URL where the SDK posts recorded locations. The SDK hosts a robust HTTP service that continuously uploads recorded locations to your server in the background, surviving app termination and  url?: string;

title: HttpEvent.responseText
text: Raw response body returned by the server. Parse this string to extract server-side data, error messages, or remote-control commands embedded in the response. responseText: string;

title: HttpEvent.status
text: HTTP status code returned by the server (e.g. , , , ). status: number;

title: HttpEvent.success
text: when the server returned a 2xx status code. success: boolean;

title: HttpMethod.Patch
text: HTTP PATCH method.

title: HttpMethod.Post
text: HTTP POST method.

title: HttpMethod.Put
text: HTTP PUT method.

title: KalmanProfile.Aggressive
text: Aggressive — Fast response; minimal smoothing.

title: KalmanProfile.Conservative
text: Conservative — Maximum smoothing; slowest response to changes.

title: KalmanProfile.Default
text: Balanced — General-purpose; suitable for most movement types.

title: Location.activity
text: Motion activity detected by the device at the time this location was recorded (e.g. , , ). activity: MotionActivity;

title: Location.age
text: Age of the location in milliseconds, measured from the device system clock at the time the location was received. + = device system time when the SDK received the location from the native API. age: number;

title: Location.battery
text: Device battery state at the time this location was recorded. battery: Battery;

title: Location.coords
text: Geographic coordinates: latitude, longitude, accuracy, speed, heading, and altitude. coords: Coords;

title: Location.event
text: SDK event that triggered this location record. One of: , , , . event?: string;

title: Location.extras
text: Optional arbitrary metadata attached to this location. Merged with configured { extras?: Record<string, any>;

title: Location.geofence
text: The geofence event that triggered this location, if applicable. Present only when { geofence?: GeofenceEvent;

title: Location.is_moving
text: when the SDK was in the moving state when this location was recorded. is_moving: boolean;

title: Location.mock
text: when the location was generated by a mock location app or simulator. mock?: boolean;

title: Location.odometer
text: Accumulated distance traveled in meters since the last odometer reset. The SDK integrates the distance between each pair of accepted locations to maintain a running total. This value survives app rest odometer: number;

title: Location.odometer_error
text: Accumulated odometer drift in meters. Represents the uncertainty that has built up in the { odometer_error: number;

title: Location.provider
text: Location-services provider state at the time this location was recorded. Present only when { provider?: ProviderChangeEvent;

title: Location.recorded_at
text: Timestamp when the SDK received the location from the native API. - When { recorded_at: string | number;

title: Location.sample
text: for intermediate sample locations collected during accuracy convergence. The SDK collects multiple samples when transitioning between motion states or during { sample?: boolean;

title: Location.timestamp
text: Timestamp provided by the native location API. - When { timestamp: string | number;

title: Location.uuid
text: Universally unique identifier for this location record. Use this to correlate locations in your server database with those in the SDK logs, or to detect whether a location has been delivered more than uuid: string;

title: LocationError.BackgroundWhenInUse
text: Attempt to initiate location-services in background with WhenInUse authorization.

title: LocationError.Cancelled
text: Location request was cancelled.

title: LocationError.LocationUnknown
text: Location could not be determined at this time.

title: LocationError.NetworkError
text: Network error occurred while attempting to fetch location.

title: LocationError.PermissionDenied
text: Location permission denied by the user.

title: LocationError.Timeout
text: Location request timed out.

title: LocationFilter.burstWindow
text: Duration in seconds of each burst window used for averaging samples. Defaults to seconds. Valid range: 0.2–120. The SDK groups all locations received within this time window into a single averaged out burstWindow?: number;

title: LocationFilter.filterDebug
text: Enables verbose debug logging for the filtering engine. Defaults to . When enabled, the SDK logs detailed decisions for each incoming sample, including whether it was ACCEPTED, REJECTED, or IGNORED by filterDebug?: boolean;

title: LocationFilter.kalmanDebug
text: Enables verbose Kalman debug output in logs. Defaults to . When enabled, the SDK logs additional diagnostic data about the Kalman filter's internal state (for example, variance and innovation) for eac kalmanDebug?: boolean;

title: LocationFilter.kalmanProfile
text: Selects the preset tuning profile for the Kalman filter. Defaults to . Each profile adjusts the Kalman filter's process and measurement noise parameters, trading off between responsiveness and smoothn kalmanProfile?: KalmanProfile;

title: LocationFilter.maxBurstDistance
text: Maximum distance in meters between samples considered part of the same burst. Defaults to meters. Valid range: 5–2000. Prevents the filter from merging location samples that are too far apart into a s maxBurstDistance?: number;

title: LocationFilter.maxImpliedSpeed
text: Maximum implied speed in meters/second permitted before a location sample is rejected. Defaults to m/s (≈ 216 km/h). Valid range: 1–200. If a raw sample implies a speed greater than this threshold — c maxImpliedSpeed?: number;

title: LocationFilter.odometerAccuracyThreshold
text: Maximum horizontal accuracy in meters allowed for a sample to affect the odometer. Defaults to meters. Valid range: 0–500. Any location whose accuracy exceeds this threshold is ignored for odometer up odometerAccuracyThreshold?: number;

title: LocationFilter.odometerUseKalmanFilter
text: Applies a Kalman filter to odometer calculations, independent of { odometerUseKalmanFilter?: boolean;

title: LocationFilter.policy
text: Selects the filtering policy applied to incoming raw GPS samples before they are accepted, averaged, or rejected. Defaults to . The filtering policy determines how aggressively the SDK removes noisy,  policy?: LocationFilterPolicy;

title: LocationFilter.rollingWindow
text: Number of samples in the rolling window used for burst averaging. Defaults to . Valid range: 3–20. Higher values produce smoother averaged locations but introduce greater latency in responsiveness to  rollingWindow?: number;

title: LocationFilter.trackingAccuracyThreshold
text: Maximum acceptable horizontal accuracy in meters for a sample to qualify for tracking. Defaults to meters. Valid range: 0–500. Locations with an accuracy value greater (worse) than this threshold are  trackingAccuracyThreshold?: number;

title: LocationFilter.useKalman
text: Enables the Kalman filter to smooth incoming location speed and position. Defaults to . When enabled, the SDK applies a Kalman filter to reduce noise and stabilize velocity and distance calculations. useKalman?: boolean;

title: LocationFilterPolicy.Adjust
text: Balanced (default) — applies moderate filtering to reject noisy samples. Dynamically adjusts acceptance thresholds for incoming samples, but never alters the raw latitude/longitude coordinates. When u

title: LocationFilterPolicy.Conservative
text: Aggressive — filters heavily, preferring stability over responsiveness.

title: LocationFilterPolicy.PassThrough
text: No filtering — accept all samples. Useful for debugging.

title: LocationRequest.Always
text: Request full background + foreground authorization.

title: LocationRequest.Any
text: Accept *either* Always or WhenInUse (no specific request).

title: LocationRequest.WhenInUse
text: Request foreground-only authorization.

title: LogLevel.Debug
text: Developer-level debug output.

title: LogLevel.Error
text: Log only critical failures.

title: LogLevel.Info
text: Operational information.

title: LogLevel.Off
text: Disable all logging.

title: LogLevel.Verbose
text: Maximum detail.

title: LogLevel.Warning
text: Log warnings + errors.

title: Logger.ORDER_ASC
text: Sort-order constant for ascending log queries (oldest first). ORDER_ASC: 1;

title: Logger.ORDER_DESC
text: Sort-order constant for descending log queries (newest first). ORDER_DESC: -1;

title: Logger.debug
text: Insert a debug-level message into the SDK's log database. debug(message: string): void;

title: Logger.destroyLog
text: Delete all records from the SDK's log database. See also - { destroyLog(): Promise<void>;

title: Logger.emailLog
text: Send the SDK's log database to an email address via the device mail client. Provide an optional { emailLog(email: string, query?: SQLQuery): Promise<boolean | void>;

title: Logger.error
text: Insert an error-level message into the SDK's log database. error(message: string): void;

title: Logger.getLog
text: Fetch the SDK's log database as a string. Provide an optional { getLog(query?: SQLQuery): Promise<string>;

title: Logger.info
text: Insert an info-level message into the SDK's log database. info(message: string): void;

title: Logger.notice
text: Insert a notice-level message into the SDK's log database. notice(message: string): void;

title: Logger.uploadLog
text: Upload the SDK's log database to a URL as a gzipped multipart file. Provide an optional { uploadLog(url: string, query?: SQLQuery): Promise<boolean | void>;

title: Logger.warn
text: Insert a warning-level message into the SDK's log database. warn(message: string): void;

title: LoggerConfig.debug
text: Enables audible soundFX and debug notifications during development. When , the SDK plays a distinct sound for each key lifecycle event so you can follow SDK activity without watching the console. It m debug?: boolean;

title: LoggerConfig.logLevel
text: Controls the verbosity of the SDK's internal log. Defaults to { logLevel?: LogLevel;

title: LoggerConfig.logMaxDays
text: Number of days to retain log entries in the internal SQLite database. Defaults to days. Entries older than this limit are purged automatically on a rolling basis. See also - { logMaxDays?: number;

title: MotionActivity.confidence
text: Confidence of the reported activity as a percentage (0–100). confidence: number;

title: MotionActivity.type
text: Detected motion activity type (e.g. , , ). type: MotionActivityType;

title: MotionActivityEvent.activity
text: Detected motion activity type (e.g. , , ). activity: MotionActivityType;

title: MotionActivityEvent.confidence
text: Confidence of the detected activity as a percentage (–). confidence: number;

title: MotionActivityType.InVehicle
text: Device is in vehicle.

title: MotionActivityType.OnBicycle
text: Device is on bicycle.

title: MotionActivityType.OnFoot
text: Device is on foot (walking or running).

title: MotionActivityType.Running
text: Device is running.

title: MotionActivityType.Still
text: Device is stationary.

title: MotionActivityType.Unknown
text: Device activity is unknown.

title: MotionActivityType.Walking
text: Device is walking.

title: MotionChangeEvent.isMoving
text: when the SDK has transitioned to the moving state; when it has transitioned to the stationary state. isMoving: boolean;

title: MotionChangeEvent.location
text: Location recorded at the moment the motion-state transition was detected. location: Location;

title: NotificationConfig.actions
text: Declare click listeners for elements of a custom notification { actions?: string[];

title: NotificationConfig.channelId
text: Identifier of the Android notification channel used for the foreground service notification. Defaults to . Note Changing this is not typically required. A use case is sharing an existing notification  channelId?: string;

title: NotificationConfig.channelName
text: Name of the Android notification channel used for the foreground service notification. Defaults to . On Android O and above, foreground services require a notification channel. The channel name is vis channelName?: string;

title: NotificationConfig.color
text: Accent color of the notification icon. Not set by default. Applies to API level 21 and above. Supported formats: - - color?: string;

title: NotificationConfig.largeIcon
text: Large icon for the foreground notification. Not set by default. ⚠️ Warning - Specify the resource type ( or ) followed by the icon name in the format . - Do not include the file extension (e.g. ). See largeIcon?: string;

title: NotificationConfig.layout
text: Name of a custom Android Layout XML file for the foreground notification. See also - [Android Custom Notification Layout](github:wiki/Android-Custom-Notification-Layout) ![](https://dl.dropbox.com/s/w layout?: string;

title: NotificationConfig.priority
text: Controls the position and visibility of the foreground notification in the system shade. Defaults to (). affects both the ordering of the notification in the notification shade and the position/visibi priority?: NotificationPriority;

title: NotificationConfig.smallIcon
text: Small status-bar icon for the foreground notification. Defaults to (the app launcher icon). ⚠️ Warning - Specify the resource type ( or ) followed by the icon name in the format . - Do not include the smallIcon?: string;

title: NotificationConfig.sticky
text: Keeps the foreground service notification visible at all times, regardless of motion state. Defaults to . By default the notification is shown only while the SDK detects the device is moving. Set to t sticky?: boolean;

title: NotificationConfig.strings
text: Custom strings to render into elements of a custom notification { strings?: Record<string, string>;

title: NotificationConfig.text
text: Body text of the foreground service notification. Defaults to . text?: string;

title: NotificationConfig.title
text: Title of the foreground service notification. Defaults to . title?: string;

title: NotificationPriority.Default
text: Default notification priority (normal weighting).

title: NotificationPriority.High
text: Notification strongly weighted to top of list; icon strongly weighted to the left.

title: NotificationPriority.Low
text: Notification weighted to bottom of list; icon weighted to the right.

title: NotificationPriority.Max
text: Same as {

title: NotificationPriority.Min
text: Notification strongly weighted to bottom of list; icon hidden.

title: PermissionRationale.message
text: Body text of the dialog. Explain why your app needs background location access, similar in purpose to the iOS . message?: string;

title: PermissionRationale.negativeAction
text: Text to display on the negative action button (e.g. "Cancel"). negativeAction?: string;

title: PermissionRationale.positiveAction
text: Text to display on the positive action button. positiveAction?: string;

title: PermissionRationale.title
text: Title for the Android background location permission dialog. title?: string;

title: PersistMode.All
text: Persist both location and geofence data.

title: PersistMode.Geofence
text: Persist geofence data only.

title: PersistMode.Location
text: Persist location data only.

title: PersistMode.None
text: Do not persist any data.

title: PersistenceConfig.disableProviderChangeRecord
text: [Android only] Disables the automatic insertion of a synthetic "provider-change" location into the SDK's SQLite database and its subsequent HTTP upload. By default, when an { disableProviderChangeRecord?: boolean;

title: PersistenceConfig.extras
text: Optional arbitrary key/value pairs merged into each recorded location at write time. These values are persisted and included in all HTTP uploads, making them ideal for attaching static contextual meta extras?: Record<string, any>;

title: PersistenceConfig.geofenceTemplate
text: Optional custom ERB template for reshaping { geofenceTemplate?: string;

title: PersistenceConfig.locationTemplate
text: Optional custom ERB template for reshaping { locationTemplate?: string;

title: PersistenceConfig.locationsOrderDirection
text: Sort order used when selecting records for upload or returning them from { locationsOrderDirection?: "ASC" | "DESC";

title: PersistenceConfig.maxDaysToPersist
text: Maximum number of days to retain a persisted record in the SDK's on-device SQLite database. Defaults to day. When your server fails to return a response, the SDK continues retrying uploads. If a recor maxDaysToPersist?: number;

title: PersistenceConfig.maxRecordsToPersist
text: Maximum number of records the SDK may retain in its on-device SQLite database. Defaults to (no limit). When a limit is set and the stored record count would exceed it, the oldest records are purged to maxRecordsToPersist?: number;

title: PersistenceConfig.persistMode
text: Controls which event types the SDK writes to its internal SQLite database. Defaults to { persistMode?: PersistMode;

title: PersistenceConfig.timestampFormat
text: Controls the format of the field in the persisted and uploaded location JSON. Because { timestampFormat?: 'iso' | 'epoch';

title: ProviderChangeEvent.accuracyAuthorization
text: Precision location authorization level. Use the { accuracyAuthorization: number;

title: ProviderChangeEvent.enabled
text: when the device has at least one location provider enabled. enabled: boolean;

title: ProviderChangeEvent.gps
text: when the GPS-based location provider is available. gps: boolean;

title: ProviderChangeEvent.network
text: when the network-based location provider is available. network: boolean;

title: ProviderChangeEvent.status
text: Platform authorization status code. Use the { status: number;

title: SQLQuery.end
text: End of the query window (unix timestamp in milliseconds). end?: number;

title: SQLQuery.limit
text: Maximum number of records to return. limit?: number;

title: SQLQuery.offset
text: Number of matching records to skip before returning results (for paging). offset?: number;

title: SQLQuery.order
text: Sort order for results: (oldest first) or (newest first). order?: SQLQueryOrder;

title: SQLQuery.start
text: Start of the query window (unix timestamp in milliseconds). start?: number;

title: SQLQueryOrder.Asc
text: Ascending order (oldest to newest).

title: SQLQueryOrder.Desc
text: Descending order (newest to oldest).

title: Sensors.accelerometer
text: when the device has an accelerometer. accelerometer: boolean;

title: Sensors.gyroscope
text: when the device has a gyroscope. gyroscope: boolean;

title: Sensors.magnetometer
text: when the device has a magnetometer (compass). magnetometer: boolean;

title: Sensors.motion_hardware
text: [iOS only] when the device has a dedicated M-series motion co-processor (iPhone 5S and later). The M-series co-processor offloads motion activity recognition from the main CPU, enabling low-power step motion_hardware?: boolean;

title: Sensors.platform
text: OS platform name: or . platform: string;

title: Sensors.significant_motion
text: [Android only] when the device supports the Significant Motion hardware trigger. When present, this sensor allows the SDK to wake from a deep idle state only when the device actually moves, saving sig significant_motion?: boolean;

title: State.activity
text: Motion recognition, stop-detection triggers, and motion-trigger delay. See { activity?: ActivityConfig;

title: State.app
text: App lifecycle — background operation, boot behaviour, headless mode, and foreground notification. See { app?: AppConfig;

title: State.authorization
text: JWT and SAS token management with automatic refresh. See { authorization?: AuthorizationConfig;

title: State.didDeviceReboot
text: when the app was launched after a device reboot. didDeviceReboot: boolean;

title: State.didLaunchInBackground
text: [iOS only] when the app was relaunched in the background by the OS — for example, due to a background fetch, geofence exit, or stationary geofence transition. On Android, background launch is handled  didLaunchInBackground: boolean;

title: State.enabled
text: when the SDK is actively tracking — i.e. { enabled: boolean;

title: State.geolocation
text: Accuracy, sampling, elasticity, stop-detection, permissions, and geofencing. See { geolocation?: GeoConfig;

title: State.http
text: Upload URL, HTTP method, sync cadence, batching, headers, and params. See { http?: HttpConfig;

title: State.isFirstBoot
text: on the very first launch after the app is installed.

title: State.isMoving
text: when the SDK is in the moving state (location services active); when stationary. isMoving: boolean;

title: State.logger
text: Debug logging, log level, and log retention. See { logger?: LoggerConfig;

title: State.odometer
text: Accumulated distance traveled since the last odometer reset, in meters. See also - { odometer: number;

title: State.odometerError
text: Accumulated positional error in the odometer measurement, in meters. Reflects noise introduced by low-accuracy location samples. Use { odometerError: number;

title: State.persistence
text: SQLite storage, TTL, record limits, and custom extras. See { persistence?: PersistenceConfig;

title: State.reset
text: Controls whether the SDK resets to factory defaults before applying this configuration. Defaults to . When (the default), every call to { reset?: boolean;

title: State.schedulerEnabled
text: when a { schedulerEnabled: boolean;

title: State.trackingMode
text: Current tracking mode. | Value | Mode | Description | |------:|------|-------------| | | Geofences | Geofence monitoring only — no active location tracking. | | | Location | Location tracking and geof trackingMode: TrackingMode;

title: State.transistorAuthorizationToken
text: Convenience option that automatically configures the SDK to upload locations to the Transistor Software demo server at tracker.transistorsoft.com, or a local instance of [background-geolocation-consol transistorAuthorizationToken?: TransistorAuthorizationToken;

text: Removes the event-listener and frees its resources. Call this when the listener is no longer needed to prevent memory leaks. remove(): void;

text: Monitor geofences only.

text: Monitor location + geofences.

text: Find or create an authorization token for the given organization and username. If a token already exists in the local cache for this + combination, the cached token is returned. Otherwise a new token  findOrCreate(orgName: string, username: string, url?: string): Promise<TransistorAuthorizationToken>;

title: TransistorAuthorizationToken.accessToken

text: Expiry time of the access token (epoch milliseconds). Typically used to populate { expires: number;

title: TransistorAuthorizationToken.refreshToken
text: JWT refresh token presented to the { refreshToken: string;

text: Target accuracy for location updates from the native API. Defaults to { desiredAccuracy?: DesiredAccuracy;

text: Optional key-value metadata to attach to each location. Merged with any configured { extras?: Record<string, any>;

title: WatchPositionRequest.interval

text: Whether to persist each location to the SDK's SQLite database and upload it to { persist?: boolean;

title: WatchPositionRequest.timeout
text: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for each location fix before firing an error. Default . timeout?: number;
